Current workflow state of the cycle count event. 'scheduled' = count is planned but not started; 'in_progress' = counting is underway; 'completed' = physical count recorded; 'reconciled' = variance reviewed and inventory adjustment posted; 'cancelled' = count was voided.

Classification of the cycle count method. 'scheduled' = routine planned count; 'ad_hoc' = unplanned triggered count; 'blind' = counter does not see system quantity; 'directed' = WMS-directed count task; 'rfid_assisted' = count performed using Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology.
The actual physical quantity of the SKU observed and recorded by the counter during the cycle count. This is the primary measured outcome of the count event.
The date and time when this cycle count record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for SOX inventory controls.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values (variance_cost, unit_cost) recorded in this cycle count record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).
The retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P06) during which the cycle count was performed. Used for period-end inventory accuracy reporting and financial close processes.
The planogram (POG) shelf position or fixture reference for the SKU at the store location, used to direct the counter to the correct physical location during in-store cycle counts.

Sequential count of how many times this SKU-location has been recounted within the same cycle count event. First count = 1; first recount = 2; etc. Supports escalation workflows when repeated recounts still show variance.

Indicates whether a recount of this SKU-location is required because the variance exceeded the tolerance threshold or the count was flagged for discrepancy. When True, a follow-up count must be performed before reconciliation.
The calendar date on which the cycle count was planned to be performed, as set in the WMS count schedule.

Classification of the root cause of inventory shrinkage identified through this cycle count. Populated during the reconciliation phase when a negative variance is confirmed. Supports shrinkage investigation and loss prevention analytics.

The specific warehouse storage bin, shelf, or slot location within the inventory node where the SKU is physically stored and counted. Granular location identifier used by the WMS for directed counting.

The inventory quantity recorded in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) or ERP at the exact time the cycle count was initiated. This is the 'book' quantity against which the physical count is compared.

Business reason that initiated this cycle count. Supports shrinkage investigation and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine|negative_inventory|shrinkage_alert|receiving_discrepancy|customer_complaint|audit_request|rfid_discrepancy — promote to reference product]
The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) unit cost of the SKU at the time the cycle count was performed, used to calculate the financial impact of any inventory variance.
The date and time when this cycle count record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.

The monetary value of the inventory variance, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the unit cost at time of count. Expressed in the local operating currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact assessment and financial reporting.

The variance quantity expressed as a percentage of the system on-hand quantity, calculated as (variance_quantity / system_quantity) * 100. Used to assess inventory accuracy and trigger shrinkage investigations when thresholds are exceeded.

The difference between the physically counted quantity and the system on-hand quantity (counted_quantity minus system_quantity). A negative value indicates shrinkage or loss; a positive value indicates an overcount or unrecorded receipt.

The maximum acceptable variance percentage for this SKU-location before a recount or inventory adjustment is triggered. Variances exceeding this threshold generate inventory_adjustment records for reconciliation.

Reference to the parent purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Enables PO-to-receipt matching, open order tracking, and three-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) for accounts payable.

Cost per unit of the received merchandise as per the purchase order or supplier invoice. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), gross margin calculation (GMROI), and financial posting to the general ledger. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the received item. Used for RFID/barcode scanning during receiving, inventory accuracy validation, and GS1 compliance.
System audit timestamp recording when this goods receipt record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, discrepancy resolution audits, and data quality monitoring.

Business-facing unique alphanumeric code identifying the reorder policy, used by replenishment planners and referenced in automated replenishment engines (e.g., Blue Yonder). Externally visible identifier across systems.
Human-readable descriptive name for the reorder policy, used by replenishment planners to identify and manage policies (e.g., 'DC Grocery Min/Max Weekly', 'Store Electronics WOS-Based').

The frequency in calendar days at which the replenishment engine evaluates this policy and checks whether a reorder trigger has been breached. Defines the periodic review interval (e.g., daily=1, weekly=7, bi-weekly=14).

Buffer stock quantity (in units) maintained above the reorder point to protect against demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. Prevents stockouts during replenishment cycles. A key input to the reorder point calculation.

Code identifying the operational system of record from which this reorder policy was originated or last updated: ORMS (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), SAP_MM (SAP S/4HANA MM Module), BLUE_YONDER (Blue Yonder Demand Planning), MANUAL (planner-entered), MDM (Informatica MDM). The desired number of weeks of inventory coverage (Weeks of Supply — WOS) to maintain at this location for this SKU. Used in wos_based reorder method. Replenishment orders are sized to achieve this WOS target based on forecasted weekly demand.
The date and time when this reorder policy record was most recently modified. Tracks parameter changes made by replenishment planners and supports change audit trails.

Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: A stock transfer is frequently initiated to fulfill a replenishment order — moving inventory from a DC to a store or between nodes as directed by the replenishment system. Linking stock_transfer to re

Reference to the replenishment plan that generated this stock transfer, linking the physical movement to the demand-driven planning signal from Blue Yonder or Oracle Retail. Supports replenishment accuracy analytics.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Inter-node stock transfers that are physically shipped generate carrier shipments. Linking stock_transfer to shipment enables tracking number association, carrier cost allocation to transfer events, a

Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being transferred. The SKU is the atomic unit of inventory tracking, uniquely identifying a product variant (size, color, style) within the retail assortment.

Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: stock_transfer.unit_of_measure is a denormalized text field. Inter-node transfers require validated UOM for transfer_cost calculations, receiving reconciliation, and variance_quantity reporting — all

Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Compared against expected_receipt_date to measure carrier and DC performance against SLA targets.
System audit timestamp recording when this stock transfer record was first persisted in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer cost is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.
Classification of the receiving inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.
Planned calendar date by which the stock transfer is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for replenishment planning, weeks of supply (WOS) calculations, and SLA compliance monitoring.

Date and time when the stock transfer record was first created in the system, representing the business event of transfer initiation. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the transfer lifecycle.

business_glossary_term: Inventory Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Per Unit
description: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value per unit for the transferred SKU at time of transfer, used for inventory valuation, GMROI calculations, and financial reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO standard cost or moving average cost.

Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is executed as a cross-docking (cross-dock) movement, where inventory is transferred directly from inbound to outbound at the DC without putaway storage. Supports DC throughput and flow-through analytics.

Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a ship-from-store (SFS) omnichannel fulfillment flow, where a retail store acts as a fulfillment node dispatching inventory to satisfy an e-commerce or BOPIS order.

Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program, where the supplier initiates and manages the replenishment transfer rather than the retailer's internal planning team.
Free-text operational notes or comments associated with the stock transfer, capturing exception details, special handling instructions, or carrier communication relevant to the transfer execution.

Operational priority assigned to the stock transfer, influencing pick sequencing, carrier selection, and dispatch scheduling at the DC or store. URGENT transfers may be triggered by stockout alerts or product recalls.

Actual number of units confirmed received at the destination node upon delivery. May differ from transfer_quantity due to transit damage, shrinkage, or carrier discrepancies. Drives inventory reconciliation and shrinkage monitoring.

Flag indicating whether this stock transfer was tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility during transit. Supports RFID-based inventory accuracy programs.
Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically dispatched from the source node. Used for lead time calculation, carrier SLA tracking, and inventory in-transit reporting.

Classification of the originating inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV or DSD; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.

Code identifying the operational system of record from which this stock transfer record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across the retail technology stack.
Carrier-assigned shipment tracking number for the stock transfer, enabling real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring. Used for exception management and carrier SLA validation.

Total logistics cost incurred for executing this stock transfer, including carrier freight charges, handling fees, and any 3PL surcharges. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), P&L allocation, and supply chain cost optimization.

Externally-known business reference number for the stock transfer, used for tracking and communication across supply chain partners, carriers, and store operations. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM transfer order numbering.
Number of units of the SKU authorized and dispatched in this stock transfer. Represents the planned transfer quantity as recorded at time of transfer creation.

Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. REPLENISHMENT: routine inventory restocking; NETWORK_BALANCE: lateral balancing to address overstock/understock; RTV: return to vendor; RECALL: product safety recall per CPSC; DAMAGE_RETURN: return of damaged goods; OMNICHANNEL: ship-from-store or BOPIS fulfillment support.

Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer. DRAFT: initiated but not yet dispatched; IN_TRANSIT: shipment dispatched from source node; RECEIVED: confirmed receipt at destination node; CANCELLED: transfer voided before completion; EXCEPTION: discrepancy or damage reported during transit.

Classification of the inter-node inventory movement. DC_TO_STORE: distribution center replenishment to store; STORE_TO_STORE: lateral balancing between stores; STORE_TO_DC: reverse logistics or consolidation; CROSS_DOCK: direct transfer without storage per cross-docking (DSD) model; RTV: Return to Vendor; MFC_TO_STORE: micro-fulfillment center to store for omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store).
12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the transferred SKU, used for scanning and verification during pick, pack, and receipt operations at DC and store nodes.

System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this stock transfer record, such as status updates, quantity adjustments, or receipt confirmations. Supports change data capture and audit compliance.

Difference between transfer_quantity and received_quantity, representing units lost, damaged, or unaccounted for during transit. Positive value indicates shortage; negative indicates overage. Drives shrinkage monitoring and carrier chargeback processes.

Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between dispatched and received units. Used for shrinkage root cause analysis, carrier chargeback (RMA) processing, and inventory reconciliation.

Unique surrogate identifier for every physical or logical inventory-holding location in the retail network (stores, DCs, MFCs, dark stores, vendor-managed locations, in-transit nodes). Primary key of this master record.

Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_zone. Business justification: Inventory nodes are assigned to cost zones that determine landed cost, freight factors, and duty rates applicable to that node. This assignment drives cost_price calculations for replenishment and goo

Add column location_id (BIGINT) to store.inventory_node with FK to store.location.location_id because inventory nodes represent physical locations. P14: connect_table: store.inventory_node** - add column location_id (BIGINT) with FK

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Every retail store/DC node belongs to a price zone that determines which sku_price records are active for that location. Price zone assignment to inventory nodes is fundamental to retail pricing execu
Primary street address of the inventory node facility. Used for carrier routing, last-mile delivery coordination, and regulatory filings.
Secondary address detail (suite, dock number, building identifier) for the inventory node facility.
City in which the inventory node is located. Used for geographic reporting, tax jurisdiction assignment, and carrier zone mapping.
Calendar date on which the inventory node was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null for active nodes. Used for historical inventory reporting and asset disposal tracking.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the node location. Governs applicable regulatory frameworks (FDA, CPSC, GDPR/CCPA), currency, and customs rules.
Timestamp when this inventory node master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports MDM audit trails and data lineage in Informatica MDM.
Scheduled frequency at which physical inventory cycle counts are conducted at this node. Drives WMS task scheduling and shrinkage monitoring cadence.
Internal district code within a region, used for field management span-of-control, district-level KPI reporting, and workforce scheduling via Workday HCM.
Number of inbound/outbound dock doors at the node. Determines receiving throughput capacity, inbound appointment scheduling windows, and cross-docking feasibility in Manhattan Associates WMS.
Retail format identifier assigned to the node (e.g., 'HYPERMARKET', 'DISCOUNT', 'DEPT_STORE', 'ECOMM_FC'). Drives assortment breadth/depth rules in ORMS and planogram (POG) assignments.
GS1-issued 13-digit Global Location Number uniquely identifying this node in the global supply chain. Used in EDI transactions (ASC X12 / EDIFACT), RFID-based receiving, and supplier communications.

business_glossary_term: Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the node supports Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) fulfillment. Drives order routing logic in the OMS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud checkout flow.
Indicates whether this node acts as a drop-ship origin point where a vendor ships directly to the end customer on behalf of the retailer, bypassing DC inventory.
Indicates whether the node is equipped with RFID infrastructure for real-time inventory tracking, cycle count automation, and shrinkage reduction.

business_glossary_term: Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the node supports Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), allowing customers to reserve items online and collect without pre-payment.
Indicates whether the node is configured as a Ship-from-Store (SFS) fulfillment center, allowing it to pick, pack, and ship e-commerce orders directly to customers.
Indicates whether this node participates in a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels.
Date of the most recent physical inventory cycle count performed at this node. Used to assess inventory accuracy, schedule next count, and monitor shrinkage compliance.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inventory node master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), MDM synchronization, and Silver Layer incremental processing in Databricks.
WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the node. Enables geospatial proximity analysis for BOPIS radius calculations, last-mile routing optimization, and store clustering.
WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the node. Used alongside latitude for geospatial routing, catchment area analysis, and omnichannel fulfillment optimization.
Name of the store manager, DC operations manager, or facility lead responsible for this inventory node. Used for escalation routing, audit accountability, and Workday HCM workforce reporting.

Maximum number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) the node can actively stock based on shelf slots, bin locations, and planogram (POG) configuration. Used in assortment planning and OTB (Open to Buy) calculations.

Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the node as maintained in SAP S/4HANA WM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Used in EDI transactions, purchase orders, and replenishment plans to reference the location. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).

Human-readable display name of the inventory node (e.g., 'Chicago Midway DC', 'Store #0421 – Oak Park'). Used in operational reports, planograms, and WMS screens. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).

Categorical classification of the inventory-holding location. Drives fulfillment capability rules, replenishment logic, and reporting hierarchies. Valid values: store (retail selling floor), distribution_center (DC), micro_fulfillment_center (MFC), dark_store (fulfillment-only), vendor_managed (VMI location), in_transit (goods in movement). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
Calendar date on which the inventory node became operationally active and began receiving or fulfilling inventory. Used for comp sales (SSS) eligibility determination and new-store ramp analysis.

Current lifecycle state of the inventory node. Controls whether the node is eligible to receive stock, fulfill orders, or appear in replenishment plans. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
Legal ownership or operating arrangement for the node facility. Drives lease accounting under FASB ASC 842, asset capitalization rules, and P&L cost allocation.
Primary operational phone number for the inventory node facility. Used for carrier coordination, inbound appointment confirmation, and emergency contact.
Postal or ZIP code of the inventory node. Used for carrier zone determination, last-mile delivery routing, and geographic market analysis.
Node code of the primary Distribution Center (DC) that replenishes this node. Used in replenishment routing, inbound appointment scheduling, and supply chain network optimization.
Internal geographic region code grouping nodes for regional P&L reporting, district management, and comp sales (SSS) analysis (e.g., 'MIDWEST', 'NORTHEAST', 'WEST_COAST').

Standard number of days from replenishment order placement to stock receipt at this node. Used by Blue Yonder Demand Planning to calculate reorder points, weeks of supply (WOS), and safety stock levels.
Minimum days of supply maintained as safety stock buffer at this node to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Feeds Blue Yonder replenishment planning and WOS calculations.
Net selling floor area in square feet, excluding back-of-house, receiving docks, and storage. Used for sales-per-square-foot KPI, planogram space allocation, and GMROI analysis.
ISO 3166-2 state or province code for the node location. Drives sales tax rules, labor law compliance, and regional reporting.

Secondary classification providing finer granularity within node_type (e.g., hypermarket, department_store, discount_outlet, cross_dock_dc, ambient_dc, cold_chain_dc, ship_from_store_hub). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hypermarket|department_store|discount_outlet|cross_dock_dc|ambient_dc|cold_chain_dc|ship_from_store_hub|ecommerce_fc — promote to reference product]
Storage temperature classification for the node. Governs which SKU categories (grocery, frozen, pharma) can be stocked and drives cold-chain compliance requirements under FDA food safety regulations.

IANA time zone identifier for the node (e.g., 'America/Chicago'). Critical for accurate timestamp normalization of POS transactions, cycle count scheduling, and SLA window calculations across multi-timezone networks.

Total physical storage area of the node measured in square feet. Used for capacity planning, space utilization analysis, and GMROI calculations. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
Unique identifier for the inventory lot or batch record. Primary key for the lot entity.

Reference to the goods receipt transaction that brought this lot into inventory. Links to receiving documentation.

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: A lot/batch is physically stored at an inventory node. lot currently has storage_location_code (a string bin/zone reference) but no FK to the inventory node where the lot resides. Adding inventory_nod
Self-referencing FK on lot (parent_lot_id)
Reference to the purchase order that sourced this lot. Links lot traceability to procurement records.
Reference to the product SKU that this lot represents. Links lot-level tracking to product master data.

Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided this lot. Critical for recall management and vendor performance tracking.

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item. Business justification: Lot traceability and product recall management require knowing which vendor item a lot originated from. Retail quality and food safety teams trace lots back to the vendor's specific item catalog entry
Current quantity available for sale or allocation from this lot. Decreases as units are sold or reserved.
Manufacturer or supplier assigned batch identifier. May differ from internal lot number for vendor-managed inventory scenarios.
Date indicating optimal quality period. Product may still be safe after this date but quality may degrade. Distinct from expiry date.
Product certification or compliance code for the lot. Examples: organic, kosher, halal, fair trade, non-GMO. Supports labeling and marketing claims.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.
Timestamp when the lot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.
Three-letter ISO currency code for unit cost. Standard codes include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, etc.
Date when the product lot expires and should no longer be sold or consumed. Mandatory for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.
Sequence number for FEFO picking logic. Lower numbers indicate earlier expiry and should be picked first to minimize waste.
Date when quality inspection was performed on the lot. Required for compliance with food safety and product safety regulations.
Detailed outcome or notes from the quality inspection process. Captures defects, non-conformances, or approval notes.
Indicates whether this lot is a private label or store brand product. True if private label; false if national brand.
Indicates whether this lot is subject to a product recall. True if recalled; false otherwise. Triggers immediate hold and customer notification.
Indicates whether this lot is part of a vendor-managed inventory agreement. True if VMI; false if retailer-owned.
Business identifier for the lot or batch. Human-readable code used for tracking and traceability across the supply chain.

Current lifecycle status of the lot. Determines whether the lot is available for sale, held for quality inspection, or restricted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|expired|recalled|disposed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date when the product lot was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf life calculation and food safety compliance.
Free-text notes or comments about the lot. Captures special handling instructions, quality observations, or operational remarks.
Quality inspection outcome for the lot. Approved lots are available for sale; rejected lots must be returned or disposed.
Total quantity of units in the lot at receipt. Used for inventory valuation and traceability reporting.
Official recall case or reference number issued by regulatory authority or manufacturer. Links to recall management system.
Date when the lot was received into inventory at the destination node. Used for aging analysis and FEFO sequencing.
Number of days remaining until expiry as of the current date. Calculated field used for near-expiry alerts and markdown triggers.
Total shelf life of the product in days from manufacture date to expiry date. Used for aging analysis and markdown planning.
Identifier of the operational system that created or owns this lot record. Examples: WMS, ERP, MDM. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
Warehouse bin, zone, or storage location where the lot is physically stored. Supports pick/pack operations and cycle counting.
Supplier's original lot or batch identifier. Used for traceability back to the supplier's manufacturing records.
Required storage temperature zone for the lot. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.
Cost per unit for this lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculation. May vary by lot due to pricing changes.
Timestamp when the lot record was last modified. Tracks changes to lot status, quantity, or other attributes for audit purposes.
Unique system-generated identifier for each inventory reservation or hold record. Primary key for the reservation data product.

Foreign key linking to ecommerce.checkout. Business justification: Reservations are created during checkout to hold inventory during payment authorization. Links reservation lifecycle to checkout session for payment-hold-release coordination and checkout abandonment

Foreign key linking to customer.corporate_account. Business justification: B2B bulk inventory reservation process: corporate accounts reserve large quantities of inventory for business purchases (e.g., corporate gifting, bulk procurement events). This is a distinct operation

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: Reservations are SKU-level allocations matching fulfillment line granularity. Retail WMS systems reserve specific SKU quantities for specific order lines during wave planning and pick task generation.

Reference to the customer order (OMS order) that triggered this inventory reservation. Populated for BOPIS, ROPIS, ship-from-store, and e-commerce hold encumbrances. Null for non-order-driven holds (quality, legal, regulatory). Links reservation to the order fulfillment lifecycle.

System identifier for the inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store) where the reserved stock physically resides. Central to ATP calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing (BOPIS, SFS, ship-from-DC).

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Inventory reservations for perishable or lot-tracked items (e.g., FEFO-based omnichannel fulfillment, recall holds) must reference the specific lot being reserved. Adding lot_id to reservation enables

Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Retail omnichannel fulfillment (BOPIS, ship-from-store, curbside pickup) requires tracking which customer profile created each inventory reservation. Essential for customer service, order fulfillment,

System identifier for the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) whose inventory is being reserved or held. Links to the product master for item-level ATP (Available to Promise) calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing.

Reference to the stock_position record at the SKU-node level that this reservation encumbers. Enables direct linkage between the reservation and the ATP calculation on the stock_position product, ensuring accurate real-time inventory visibility.

Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: reservation.unit_of_measure is a denormalized text field. Inventory reservations for BOPIS/ROPIS/fulfillment require validated UOM to correctly decrement available_to_promise quantities and prevent ov

Name or code of the business department that authorized the hold (e.g., Quality Assurance, Legal, Compliance, Store Operations, E-Commerce Fulfillment). Supports departmental accountability reporting and hold resolution workflows.

Reference to the service case, quality investigation, legal matter, or regulatory recall case that authorized a hard hold. Populated for quality_hold, legal_hold, regulatory_recall, and damage_hold encumbrance types. Links to Salesforce Service Cloud case management.

System-generated timestamp when the reservation record was first written to the data platform (Silver layer ingestion time). Distinct from reservation_timestamp which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory_cost_per_unit value (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for international retail operations.

Classifies the nature of the inventory encumbrance. Soft allocations (e.g., BOPIS reservation, ship-from-store allocation, e-commerce hold) are temporary and customer-driven; hard holds (quality hold, regulatory recall, legal hold, damage hold, price investigation) restrict stock from sale pending resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: soft_allocation|hard_hold|quality_hold|regulatory_recall|legal_hold|damage_hold|price_investigation — promote to reference product]

Date and time at which the reservation automatically expires if not fulfilled or manually extended. Critical for BOPIS and e-commerce holds where stock must be released back to general availability after a defined window (e.g., 24–48 hours). Null for indefinite holds such as legal or regulatory holds.

Retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P07) during which the reservation was created. Supports financial reporting of encumbered inventory valuation, OTB (Open to Buy) impact analysis, and period-end inventory reconciliation per FASB ASC 330.

The omnichannel fulfillment method associated with this reservation. BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), ship_from_store (SFS), ecommerce (ship from DC), drop_ship (vendor direct), in_store (POS hold). Null for non-order-driven hard holds.

Standardized code identifying the specific business reason for the reservation or hold (e.g., BOPIS_ALLOC, SFS_ALLOC, ECOM_HOLD, QA_FAIL, RECALL_CPSC, LEGAL_HOLD, DMG_INBOUND, PRICE_INVEST). Enables operational reporting and root-cause analysis of inventory encumbrances.

Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for the inventory hold or reservation, providing operational context beyond the structured hold_reason_code. Required for legal holds and regulatory recalls per compliance documentation standards.

Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for the reserved SKU at the time of reservation creation. Used for financial valuation of encumbered inventory, shrinkage reporting, and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO module.

Indicates whether this reservation was created in response to a regulatory product recall (CPSC, FDA, or other governing body). When true, the hold cannot be released without explicit compliance authorization. Supports mandatory recall reporting and quarantine workflows.

Indicates whether the reserved inventory is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement, where the supplier retains ownership and replenishment responsibility. Affects financial liability and hold authorization workflows.

Business priority assigned to the reservation, governing queue position in fulfillment workflows and hold resolution SLAs. 'critical' applies to regulatory recalls and legal holds; 'high' to same-day BOPIS; 'standard' to regular e-commerce holds; 'low' to pre-emptive soft allocations.

Free-text field capturing resolution details, disposition instructions, or compliance notes recorded at the time of hold release. Required for regulatory recalls and legal holds to document the resolution rationale for audit purposes.

Approval workflow status for releasing a hard hold. 'pending' — release request submitted awaiting authorization; 'approved' — release authorized by responsible department; 'rejected' — release denied, hold remains active; 'auto_released' — system-triggered release upon expiry or order fulfillment; 'escalated' — requires senior authorization.

Actual date and time when the reservation was released, cancelled, or converted. Null while the reservation is active. Used for hold duration analysis, SLA compliance reporting, and ATP recalculation upon release.

Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the reservation, used in operational communications, order management, and customer-facing references (e.g., BOPIS confirmation codes). Sourced from OMS or WMS upon creation.

Current lifecycle state of the inventory reservation. 'active' — stock is currently encumbered; 'expired' — reservation lapsed past expiry without fulfillment; 'released' — hold manually or systematically lifted; 'cancelled' — reservation voided before fulfillment; 'pending_release' — awaiting authorization to release; 'converted' — soft allocation fulfilled and converted to a shipment.

The precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the inventory encumbrance was created — the principal business event timestamp. For BOPIS/e-commerce holds, this is when the customer completed checkout. For quality/legal holds, this is when the hold was authorized.

Number of units of the SKU encumbered by this reservation. Subtracted from on-hand quantity to derive ATP (Available to Promise) on the stock_position product. Must be positive and not exceed available on-hand at time of reservation creation.

Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this reservation record (e.g., WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS, OMS = Order Management System, ECOM = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, ERP = SAP S/4HANA, MANUAL = manually entered hold). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WMS|OMS|ECOM|ERP|ORMS|CAR|SFCC|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Specific bin, shelf, or slot location within the node where the reserved inventory is physically stored. Enables warehouse associates to locate and pick reserved stock efficiently. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS bin management.
12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the reserved item. Used for RFID scanning, POS reconciliation, and regulatory recall identification per GS1 standards.

System-generated timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, expiry extension). Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each stock ledger transaction record. Primary key for the perpetual inventory ledger at the SKU-location-date level.
